name: New Yam Festival (Iri Ji)
traditions: Offering the first yams to ancestors and deities; communal feasting; masquerade dances; wrestling matches; palm wine drinking.
historicalBackground: The New Yam Festival (Iri Ji Ohuru) predates colonialism and marks the end of a farming cycle. Yam is the king of crops in Igbo culture.
description: The Igbo harvest thanksgiving, celebrating the sacred first yam of the season with ancestral offerings, masquerades, and communal feasts.
